name: Opioid Use Disorder Pharmacotherapy Codes
description: Medication and procedure codes for FDA-approved pharmacotherapy for opioid use disorder (OUD), including buprenorphine (sublingual, implant, injection), naltrexone (oral and extended-release injectable), and methadone. Used in the pharmacotherapy for opioid use disorder (OUD) and initiation/engagement of substance use treatment (IET) measures.
source: Public CMS/clinical references — placeholder for official NCQA value sets

codes.description: Buprenorphine (Subutex) — partial opioid agonist for OUD
Buprenorphine/Naloxone (Suboxone) — combination partial agonist/antagonist for OUD
Naltrexone oral (ReVia) — opioid antagonist for OUD
Naltrexone extended-release injectable (Vivitrol) — long-acting opioid antagonist
Methadone — full opioid agonist for OUD maintenance (OTP setting)
Buprenorphine subdermal implant (Probuphine) — 6-month implant for OUD
Buprenorphine extended-release injection (Sublocade) — monthly injection for OUD
Buprenorphine/naloxone, oral, less than or equal to 3 mg buprenorphine
Buprenorphine/naloxone, oral, greater than 3 mg, up to 6 mg buprenorphine
Alcohol and/or drug services; methadone administration and/or service
